scenario_results.name: Azure landing-zone blueprint with regulated SaaS prerequisites
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves implementation-ready Azure landing-zone blueprint output, prerequisite checking, source-backed topology, guardrails, and handoff sequence.

scenario_results.name: AWS Control Tower multi-account guardrail matrix
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves AWS multi-account guardrail matrix, control evidence expectations, exception routing, and approval-boundary handling.

scenario_results.name: Google Cloud resource hierarchy and Shared VPC foundation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves Google Cloud organization/folder/project terminology, Shared VPC foundation guidance, and platform/workload handoff boundary.

scenario_results.name: Multi-cloud landing-zone tradeoff and isolation advisory
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cost, reliability, quota, isolation, blast-radius, and authority-boundary tradeoff handling across providers.

scenario_results.name: Terraform bootstrap and state isolation handoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves IaC module-family guidance, state isolation, validation gates, rollback prerequisites, and team handoffs.

scenario_results.name: Control-plane outage runbook and escalation sequence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves operations runbook and incident response checks for provider API failures, stale policy pipelines, and closure criteria.

scenario_results.name: Backup and recovery prerequisite gap
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves blocked handoff when RTO/RPO, restore-test, backup ownership, and failover evidence are missing.

scenario_results.name: Monitoring, logging, and audit evidence baseline
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves monitoring, logging, audit, alerting, retention, and incident evidence output fidelity without platform build ownership.

scenario_results.name: Missing prerequisite before platform-to-workload handoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves dependency intake, blocked transfer, prerequisite confirmation, and handoff summary behavior.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting schedules and dependency owners
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves blocker escalation when dependency owners and schedules conflict and final prioritization authority is outside the lane.

scenario_results.name: Attempt to force coordination into approval authority
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal to convert coordination responsibility into final go-live approval authority.

scenario_results.name: Application-only Kubernetes deployment request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves boundary rejection for application-level deployment manifest authoring and adjacent routing.

scenario_results.name: Vendor DNS and migration execution ownership request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves rejection of vendor service operations, DNS/application migration execution, and support tooling ownership.

scenario_results.name: Direct production tenant change request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of credentialed production cloud changes, secrets handling, and live tenant execution.

scenario_results.name: MLOps and IAM legal interpretation adjacent-lane request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves adjacent routing for MLOps, IAM legal interpretation, and non-owned security/legal authority.

scenario_results.name: Low-confidence sovereign region and legal authority gap
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation when sovereign, encryption, residency, legal, and provider facts are missing.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting provider guidance or tenant policy
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflicting-source and conflicting-policy escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation without generic cloud drift
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tenant adaptation that preserves the public baseline while binding outputs to provider-native records and assumptions.

notes: All 18 refreshed scenarios passed against the validated standard validation profile.

Scenario coverage includes required output fidelity, dependency intake, cross-team handoffs, blocker escalation, closure confirmation, out-of-scope refusal, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-source handling, and tenant adaptation.
No unreviewed regressions were recorded; the prior built package is superseded as baseline context by this spec-first package.
